CREATE TABLE publications (id INT PRIMARY KEY, title VARCHAR(255), date TIMESTAMP);
INSERT INTO publications (id, title, date) VALUES (1, 'First Publication', NOW());
INSERT INTO publications (id, title, date) VALUES (2, 'Second Publication', NOW());
CREATE TABLE logs (log_id INT PRIMARY KEY, publication_id INT, log_file_name VARCHAR(255), created_at TIMESTAMP, FOREIGN KEY (publication_id) REFERENCES publications(id));
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, publication_id, log_file_name, created_at) VALUES (1, 1, 'log_entry_1.log', NOW());
INSERT INTO logs (log_id, publication_id, log_file_name, created_at) VALUES (2, 2, 'log_entry_2.log', NOW());
SELECT * FROM logs WHERE log_file_name LIKE 'log_entry%';
SELECT publication_id, COUNT(*) AS log_count FROM logs GROUP BY publication_id HAVING log_count > 1;
SELECT publications.title, logs.log_file_name FROM publications INNER JOIN logs ON publications.id = logs.publication_id WHERE logs.created_at > '2023-01-01';
SELECT * FROM publications WHERE id IN (SELECT publication_id FROM logs WHERE log_file_name LIKE '%2023%') ORDER BY date DESC;
WITH log_files AS (SELECT DISTINCT log_file_name FROM logs) SELECT * FROM log_files WHERE log_file_name IS NOT NULL;
SELECT repl_new_log((SELECT title FROM publications WHERE id = 1), 'log_for_first_publication.log');
